research.status_summary: Canva is actively hiring (300+ open roles globally as of Jan 2026) while simultaneously executing selective AI-driven restructuring — notably absorbing Leonardo.AI's ~150-person team into its core AI org and cutting a small technical writing team in Apr 2025 amid broader AI substitution pressure; COO Cliff Obrecht's deleted March 2026 Slack note comparing Canva's ~5,000-person headcount to Anthropic's ~2,300 sparked internal layoff fears, though no mass cuts have been confirmed.
research.hiring_policy: Canva (HQ: Sydney) offers visa sponsorship for software engineering, product management, and design roles, primarily for its Australian offices; for US roles, Canva Us Inc. has filed H-1B LCAs (marketing managers, sales, software developers, data analysts) but is a modest US sponsor. Remote/flexible work is a stated policy ('work from home allowed, hours flexible, what matters is getting work done'), making it hybrid-friendly though not fully EOR-remote by default.
recent_news: Canva AI 2.0 launched Apr 16, 2026 at Canva Create in LA — billed as the platform's biggest evolution since 2013, introducing a proprietary Canva Design Model (world's first design-specific foundation model), conversational/agentic editing, persistent memory, and six new workflows (Connectors, Scheduling, Web Research, Brand Intelligence, Sheets AI, Canva Code 2.0).

Canva acquired Ortto (Apr 8, 2026) and earlier acquired Cavalry + MangoAI (Feb 2026), integrating motion graphics and AI video-ad optimization into its suite; Leonardo.AI fully absorbed into Canva's 100+ AI product/research teams (Mar 2026) — bringing total acquisitions to 5+ in two years.

Revenue hit $4B ARR by end of 2025 (+20% MAU growth to 265M users, 31M paid); B2B segment (25+ seats) grew 100% YoY reaching $500M ARR; Canva fined A$792K in May 2026 for failing to lodge financial results on time; IPO signaled by COO as 'next couple of years.'
research.growth_signal: Canva is on a steep upward trajectory — $4B ARR, 265M MAUs, 100% enterprise growth YoY, ~15,755 employees as of Apr 2026, a $42B–$65B valuation range, and a wave of acquisitions and the landmark Canva AI 2.0 platform launch positioning it as the dominant Creative AI OS for non-designers and marketing teams.
research.fit_for_lior: Canva is a direct bull's-eye for Lior's Creative AI + Product Analyst threads — Canva AI 2.0's agentic design workflows, Magic Layers, and LLM integrations (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity) are exactly the 'vibe coding meets marketing design' surface she covers, and Canva is a confirmed sponsor actively hiring product, AI, and growth roles globally.
